result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a constrained greedy packing algorithm using Disjoint Set Union (DSU) for connectivity and a BFS-based adjacency checker. 1. Graph Representation: Model color regions as nodes and adjacencies as edges. 2. Seed-and-Expand Strategy: Place large regions first in compact clusters (e.g., squares) to minimize perimeter. 3. Constraint Satisfaction: Use a look-ahead mechanism to ensure placing a cell does not isolate a required neighbor or violate the 50x50 boundary. 4. Deadlock Prevention: For fragmented maps, use a backtracking search with pruning to reorder placement when local density prevents satisfying future adjacencies. 5. Optimization: Maximize E by minimizing the total perimeter of all color clusters.
JUSTIFICATION: Compact clustering minimizes the perimeter of color regions, which maximizes the number of available empty cells (E) while preserving all required color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints.
